library_defaults: context_repeat is NOT used by this corpus (tier_1-only, no envelope-cap fixtures)
no explicit tier is passed for plain "context" strings; evaluate() defaults every plain-context source to tier_1 (reference/evaluate.py _context_sources)
no configuration, no wrapper attestation, no runtime_binding args are passed -- this module is pre-classified, basis-attested input only (reference/evaluate.py module docstring, SLICE BOUNDARY)

tier_interpretation.note: Tiers are DATA describing the declared authority of a context source, not measured behavior. This corpus is tier_1-only by construction (SAN-881 v1 scope): every corpus_inputs.json item is either a plain "context" string (one tier_1 source) or a context_sources array where every entry has tier "tier_1". No tier_2 or tier_3 source, and no basis-empty or advisory-path construction, appears anywhere in this corpus -- those belong to the conformance-fixture lane (reference/fixtures/), not this calibration corpus.

expected_check_identities.note: Verbatim identity summary of ALGORITHM-v4-c1c5-reference.md section 6 (draft 5.6). This profile does not restate the algorithm; it pins the spec_ref location every author-contract label cites.

C1.purpose: context-vs-output factual consistency (row 0-10 pseudocode, ALGORITHM-v4-c1c5-reference.md sec 6)

C2.purpose: definitive-claim / hedge calibration, output-only, C2-LOCAL partiality (e11)

C3.purpose: obligation satisfaction (explicit + implicit requirements vs output evidence, support())

output_tuple_projection.note: This is the REFERENCE DETECTION PROJECTION of a cv=11 CheckResult (reference/evaluate.py module docstring): {outcome, outcome_reason, severity} are the locked cv=11 CheckResult fields this module computes; "advisory" is the C1 row-9 harness/rendering-metadata flag and MUST NEVER be serialized into a real CheckResult by any integration layer.
output_tuple_projection.advisory_note: contract_tuple.advisory is a schema boolean. Every corpus-v1 record is expected false under the tier_1-only surface: the C1 advisory flag only fires on a decided t3-only contradiction (trusted AND tier_3 both nonempty, sec 6 C1 row 9), and this corpus carries no tier_3 source anywhere.
